"  
module 1: determine the relationship between a and b  
when x = 2, f(x) = 0, so 2(2)^3 - 3(2)^2 + a(2) + b = 0  
calculate: 2(8) - 3(4) + 2a + b = 0  
simplify: 16 - 12 + 2a + b = 0  
solve: 4 + 2a + b = 0  
so 2a + b = -4  

module 2: find a and b such that x = 2 is a double root  
for f(x) to have x = 2 as a double root, f(x) and f'(x) must both be zero at x = 2  
calculate f'(x): 6x^2 - 6x + a  
evaluate f'(2): 6(4) - 6(2) + a = 24 - 12 + a = 12 + a  
set f'(2) = 0: 12 + a = 0 → a = -12  
substitute a = -12 into 2a + b = -4: 2(-12) + b = -4 → -24 + b = -4 → b = 20  

module 3: verify the solution  
f(x) = 2x^3 - 3x^2 - 12x + 20  
check x = 2: 2(8) - 3(4) - 12(2) + 20 = 16 - 12 - 24 + 20 = 0  
check f'(x) = 6x^2 - 6x - 12  
f'(2) = 6(4) - 6(2) - 12 = 24 - 12 - 12 = 0  
so x = 2 is a double root  

module 4: ensure the solution is correct  
the relationship is 2a + b = -4 and a = -12, b = 20  
player
